evidence: app/app.py: no authentication or authorization — the Shiny app is a read-only data exploration tool with no user accounts, no write operations, and no sensitive data mutations; this is appropriate for a public research tool
app/app.py download_csv: generates a client-side base64 data URI — no server-side file write, no path traversal risk
app/app.py load_data(): fetches from a hardcoded public GitHub URL — no user-controlled URL input, no SSRF risk
IDR/idr_analysis.py: fetches from UniProt REST API (hardcoded URL template with only the accession substituted from the dataset) — accession values come from the curated DataFrame, not user input
No route handlers, no RLS, no permission strings — this is a data analysis tool, not a multi-user web application

strengths: The application is read-only with no authentication surface — appropriate for a public research data explorer
No user-controlled inputs are used to construct file paths or URLs
Data is loaded from a single hardcoded trusted source (GitHub raw URL)
authorization_consistency.issue_character: No authorization defects found — the application has no authentication surface by design; the only minor concern is that analysis scripts contain hardcoded Windows dev paths that would fail on other systems, but this is not an authorization issue.
authorization_consistency.score_rationale: This is a read-only research data tool with no user accounts, no write operations, and no sensitive data. Authorization consistency is not applicable in the traditional sense. The score reflects that no authorization anti-patterns were found, docked slightly because the app has no documented statement of its public/unauthenticated stance for future maintainers.

possible_false_positives: Any authorization findings here would be false positives — the tool is intentionally public and read-only
